name: opl-cancer
description: OPL for Cancer (CancerDAO) — a patient's own AI scientist team (PI Sid + 20 expert archetypes + IRB-substitute auditor Henry) producing a PMID-anchored, three-tier-labelled research brief — not treatment advice; the patient is sole decision authority. Use when a cancer patient or caregiver has records (PDF/images/folder/zip) and wants research-grade analysis: next-line options, NGS interpretation, clinical-trial matching, expanded-access / cross-border navigation, hypothesis generation, drug repurposing, public-dataset re-analysis, or a second look at a treatment plan.

interface.shortDescription: A patient's own AI scientist team — PMID-anchored research brief, not treatment advice.
interface.longDescription: OPL for Cancer (CancerDAO) gives one patient one PI (Sid) coordinating a 20-expert virtual lab plus an IRB-substitute auditor (Henry), running a 5-Wave research lifecycle from records-in to patient-brief-out — every claim PMID-anchored, provenance-hashed, three-tier-labelled, and reproducible. Not a clinical decision-support tool, not a diagnostic device, not a doctor-replacement. The patient is the sole decision authority. Use it for next-line options, NGS interpretation, clinical-trial matching, expanded-access / cross-border navigation, hypothesis generation, drug repurposing, public-dataset re-analysis, or a second look at a treatment plan.
defaultPrompt: I have my father's medical records — run a full research analysis as my AI scientist team.
Standard treatment is exhausted; what next-line options does the evidence support?
Interpret this NGS report and find matching clinical trials.
Generate and rank research hypotheses for my N=1 case.
